lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The chapel at St. Brendan's has a door that opens onto the ER corridor — a design choice nobody planned and nobody has corrected. Sister Agnes uses it the way a cardiologist uses a stethoscope. You stand at the threshold long enough and the unit tells you things.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Tonight it is telling her about Clive Harte.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He has made three passes in forty minutes. Not the restless orbit of a man managing a crisis — that has a different quality, faster, eyes on charts. This is surveillance. He slows each time near bay seven. Bay seven is Theo Okafor's station.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She notes it the way she notes everything: without judgment, without urgency. The ER is a body with its own circulatory system, and she has learned to read pressure gradients. Harte's movement is constriction. Something upstream is being squeezed.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo hasn't looked up. That's the tell — not fear exactly, but the careful performance of a man who has already felt the weight of being watched and decided the safest answer is to give them nothing to read.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Agnes knows that posture. It lasts until it doesn't.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Harte stops at the nursing station, exchanges three words with the charge nurse, then turns. For a moment his sight line runs straight to the chapel doorway. Agnes does not step back.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He looks away first. They always do.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She pulls the door to. Not closed — just narrowed. Enough to watch through. The shift has a long way to run, and Theo is going to need somewhere to go when it finally catches up with him.
